content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration · · · Audit Counsel · · · 322 5901 · · · Compliance · · · 322 6441

(916) 322 5860

October 3, 1978

R. T. L. Littlefair
5512 Calle de Ricardo
Torrance, CA 90505

Dear Mr. Littlefair:

This is in response to your letter of September 13, 1978
regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act.

The Political Reform Act of 1974 does not prohibit any
person from holding government office. The Act does, however,
require that a public official disqualify himself from making
or participating in making a governmental decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect on, among other things, an source of income
to the official aggregating $250 or more in the year preceding
the decision.

Because of the community property nature of income earned
by married persons in California, the Commission has advised
officials that they must, in general, disqualify themselves
in any situation where their decision would have a material
financial effect on a source of income to their spouse aggregating $500 or more in the previous year. If the school district
were a source of income to your wife, as defined by the Act,
you would therefore have to refrain from participating in any
decision which would have a material financial effect on the
school district. Although the Act would not prohibit you
from serving on the board, it would greatly restrict your
participation in the board's activities.

The definition of "income" included in the Act, however,
specifically excludes salary and reimbursements received from
local government agencies or from bona fide educational

R. T. L. Littlefair                                                Page Two
October 3, 1978


organizations.  See Government Code §82030(b)(2).  Since
your community property share of your wife's income is not
"income" within the meaning of the Act, your wife's status
as a teacher in the district would create no conflict of
interest for you as a member of the board.

   If you have further questions concerning the applicability
of the Political Reform Act, please feel free to call me at
(916) 322-6444.

                                          Sincerely,

                                          [signature]
                                          Sarah Cameron
                                          Counsel
                                          Conflicts of Interest Division

STC:nrw

R.T.L. Littlelair
5512 CALLE DE RICARDO
TORRANCE CALIFORNIA 90505
PHONE 375-2652

EPPC
SEP 15 11 37 AM '78

September 13, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

I propose to be a candidate for election to the Torrance
Unified School District Board at the regular election
during March, 1979.

My wife is a tenured teacher in the School District and I
would appreciate receiving written confirmation that there
would be no conflict of interest if I were elected to the
School Board.

Sincerely,

R. T. L. Littlefair

RTLL:ams
